ai.summary: SpeakMove — Telegram-бот для изучения языка с голосовыми диалогами на основе AI. На старте — английский; немецкий и норвежский в roadmap. Пользователи получают монеты (SM) за каждый урок и обменивают их на подарочные карты Amazon или Visa Prepaid (курс: 100 SM = $1, минимальный обмен $10; $5 для первых 200 в раннем доступе). Для пользователей из Украины cashback заменён скидкой −30% на Premium. Структура обучения построена по системе CEFR (A1–C1). Сейчас SpeakMove — research-проект, оператор: Dmytro Fenko, UK sole trader (ICO № ZC141876). При запуске коммерческого продукта возможна смена формы на UK Ltd с уведомлением подписчиков waitlist.

faq.question: Lorem ipsum SpeakMove 158
faq.answer: SpeakMove — это Telegram-бот для изучения языка с голосовыми AI-диалогами. На старте — английский. За каждый урок пользователь получает монеты (SM), которые на тарифе Premium можно обменять на подарочные карты Amazon или Visa Prepaid. Курс — 100 SM = $1; за календарный месяц начисляется до 30% от стоимости подписки. Для пользователей из Украины cashback заменён прямой скидкой 30% только на Premium (Plus идёт по обычной цене — на нём cashback и так не предусмотрен).

faq.question: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet SpeakMove. Dolor sit amet.
faq.answer: За каждый голосовой диалог начисляется +5 SM на Plus и +10 SM на Premium; за домашние задания (только Premium) — те же +10 SM; за 7 дней подряд — бонус +25 SM (Plus) / +50 SM (Premium); за 30 дней подряд — +100 / +200 SM. Курс: 100 SM = $1. За календарный месяц начисляется максимум 30% от стоимости месячной подписки — защита, чтобы cashback оставался стабильным для всех. Минимальный обмен — $10 (1 000 SM); для первых 200 пользователей в раннем доступе — $5 (500 SM). Обмен доступен только на тарифе Premium. Варианты подарочных карт: Amazon (DE / UK / .COM) или Visa Prepaid (универсальная). Монеты живут 12 месяцев с момента начисления. Для пользователей из Украины cashback заменён скидкой 30% на Premium.

faq.question: Lorem ipsum SpeakMove 159
faq.answer: Бесплатный план: 1 диалог в день, без накопления монет. Plus: $12.99/мес в раннем доступе ($16.99/мес после первых 200) — расширенная практика, прогресс по CEFR, сертификат при завершении уровня. Premium: $22.99/мес в раннем доступе ($28.99/мес после первых 200) — всё из Plus + ДЗ с x2 монет, cashback в gift cards (Amazon или Visa Prepaid). Полный родительский дашборд появится в V1.1; в первом релизе родитель получает еженедельный email-отчёт о прогрессе ребёнка. Для пользователей из Украины — скидка −30% на Premium-планы. Plus (месячный и годовой) идёт по обычной цене. Скидочная цена раннего доступа фиксируется на 3 месяца с момента запуска.

Курс монет: 100 SM = $1, минимум для обмена $10 ($5 для первых 200), комиссия $0. Программа для Украины: cashback заменён скидкой −30% только на Premium. Оплата банковской картой (Stripe) или через Telegram Stars. Отмена в один тап. Сейчас SpeakMove — research-проект; оператор Dmytro Fenko, UK sole trader (ICO № ZC141876). ai.summary: Страница вейтлиста SpeakMove — формы регистрации в раннем доступе. Сейчас SpeakMove — research-проект; оператор Dmytro Fenko, UK sole trader (ICO № ZC141876). Доступ ограничен 200 пользователями: они фиксируют скидочные тарифы (Plus $12.99/мес вместо $16.99, Premium $22.99/мес вместо $28.99) на 3 месяца с момента официального запуска. Регистрация требует имя, email, сегмент (родитель ребёнка 6–14, взрослый для себя или оба) и страну (для определения тарифной программы — cashback в gift cards Amazon Gift Card / Visa Prepaid или скидка −30% для пользователей из Украины только на Premium; Plus в UA идёт по обычной цене). Поля «фамилия» и «целевой язык» необязательные. Карту вводить не нужно — оплата только при активации Plus или Premium после запуска. Первые 200 пользователей не платят за раннюю регистрацию. Уведомление об открытии доступа приходит на email одним письмом. После регистрации пользователь видит свой номер в очереди и может отписаться в один клик. meta.title: Privacy Policy
meta.intro: This policy explains what personal data SpeakMove collects when you visit our website or join our waitlist, why we collect it, and what rights you have. We keep it short and in plain English.

sections.heading: 1. Who we are
body: This research project is operated by Dmytro Fenko (publishing as Dmitry Fenko), a UK sole trader trading as SpeakMove. I am the data controller for your personal data. The project does not meet the conditions in UK GDPR Article 37 that require a Data Protection Officer, so I have not appointed one — I handle every data protection matter personally.

I am registered with the UK Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) as a data controller and pay the annual data protection fee. My ICO registration number is ZC141876. You can verify it on the public register at ico.org.uk/ESDWebPages/Search.
Address for service of documents: Weston-super-Mare, UK, BS23 1RH.
Contact for privacy matters: hi@speakmove.app

sections.heading: 2. What data we collect
body: When you use our website or sign up to the waitlist, we may collect:

items: Email address (required to join the waitlist)
First name (required for the waitlist form)
Last name (optional)
Preferred language and user segment (optional, chosen from a dropdown)
IP address and browser metadata (collected automatically by our hosting provider and error tracking tool)
Google Analytics identifiers (e.g. _ga cookie) for visitor analytics
Sentry error context (e.g. browser type, operating system, stack traces — for debugging)

body: We do not collect payment details, government IDs, or any special category data.

sections.heading: 3. Why we collect it and our lawful basis
body: We rely on the following lawful bases under UK GDPR Article 6 (and EU GDPR Article 6 for EU/EEA users):

items: Consent (Art. 6(1)(a)): We ask for your explicit consent when you submit the waitlist form. We use your email and name solely to notify you when SpeakMove launches. You can withdraw consent at any time.

Legitimate interests (Art. 6(1)(f)): We use Google Analytics to understand how visitors use our site, and Sentry to catch technical errors. We have assessed that our legitimate interest in maintaining a working, secure site does not override your interests or rights.

sections.heading: 4. How long we keep your data

items: Waitlist data (email, name, segment): until SpeakMove launches and we send our notification email, or until you withdraw consent — whichever comes first. We then delete your data within 90 days.
Google Analytics data: 14 months maximum (we use GA4's minimum retention setting).
Sentry error logs: 30 days.

sections.heading: 5. Who we share your data with
body: We share your data with the following third-party processors only where necessary:

items: Google LLC (Google Analytics 4) — visitor analytics. Data processed in the US.
Sentry (Functional Software, Inc.) — error tracking. Data processed in the US.
Vercel Inc. — website hosting and delivery. Data processed in the US and globally via edge network.
Hetzner Cloud GmbH (Germany) — VPS hosting (database for waitlist submissions). Data resides within the EU.
Telegram Messenger — our Telegram bot is registered but not yet functional. It responds with an automated message directing users to our waitlist.

body: We do not sell your personal data to anyone, ever.

sections.heading: 6. International data transfers
body: Some of our third-party processors are based outside the UK and EU. Here is how we protect your data in each case:

items: Google Analytics (US): transferred under the UK Extension to the EU–US Data Privacy Framework, recognised as adequate by the UK.
Sentry (US): transferred under a UK International Data Transfer Agreement (IDTA) or UK Addendum to EU Standard Contractual Clauses.
Vercel (US/global edge): transferred under UK IDTA.
Hetzner Cloud GmbH (Germany, EU) — data resides within the EU/EEA, no third-country transfer applies.
Telegram (international): we rely on controller-to-processor safeguards as appropriate.

body: If your data originates in the EU/EEA, it first lands in the UK (which holds EU adequacy status) and is then subject to the mechanisms listed above for any onward transfer.

sections.heading: 7. Your rights
body: Under UK GDPR (and EU GDPR for EU/EEA residents), you have the right to:

items: Access your data (Art. 15) — ask us what we hold about you.
Correct inaccurate data (Art. 16).
Delete your data (Art. 17) — also known as the right to be forgotten.
Restrict how we use your data (Art. 18).
Receive your data in a portable format (Art. 20).
Object to processing based on legitimate interests (Art. 21).
Withdraw consent at any time (Art. 7(3)) — this does not affect the lawfulness of processing before withdrawal.

body: To exercise any of these rights, email us at hi@speakmove.app. We will respond within one calendar month.

sections.heading: 8. How to complain
body: If you are unhappy with how we handle your data, please contact us first at hi@speakmove.app and we will try to resolve it.
UK users: you also have the right to complain to the UK Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) — ico.org.uk.
EU/EEA users: you may complain directly to your national data protection authority. We have no EU establishment, so there is no single lead supervisory authority. You can contact:

items: Germany: Bundesbeauftragter für den Datenschutz und die Informationsfreiheit (BfDI) — bfdi.bund.de
France: Commission Nationale de l'Informatique et des Libertés (CNIL) — cnil.fr
Norway: Datatilsynet — datatilsynet.no

sections.heading: 9. We do not sell your data
body: We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal data to any third party.

Это не относится к нашим техническим подрядчикам (data processors) — их полный список и роли указаны в разделе «Кому мы передаём данные» выше. Они обрабатывают данные строго по нашим инструкциям, в рамках Data Processing Agreement (DPA), и не имеют права использовать их в собственных целях.
Также мы можем раскрыть данные, если этого требует закон (судебный приказ, запрос компетентного органа) или для защиты наших прав в случае мошенничества и злоупотреблений.

sections.heading: 10. Age limit
body: Our waitlist and website are intended for users aged 16 and over. We apply the highest age threshold across our operating countries (16, as required in Germany and by EU standards). If you believe someone under 16 has submitted their data, please contact us at hi@speakmove.app and we will delete it promptly.

sections.heading: 11. Future company migration
body: We intend to incorporate SpeakMove as a UK limited company before launching paid features. If we do so, your personal data and our obligations under this policy will transfer to the successor entity.

We will give you at least 30 days' prior notice by email before any such transfer. You will have the right to object — including the right to request deletion of your data — before the transfer takes place.

sections.heading: 12. Changes to this policy
body: If we make material changes to this policy, we will notify waitlist members by email at least 14 days before the changes take effect. The 'Last updated' date at the top of this page will always reflect the most recent revision.

sections.heading: 13. Contact
body: For any privacy-related questions: hi@speakmove.app

meta.title: Terms of Service
meta.intro: These terms govern your use of the SpeakMove website and waitlist. Please read them — they are short and written in plain English.

sections.heading: 1. Pre-launch status
body: SpeakMove is currently in pre-launch. There is no functional product available. The website provides information about the planned service and allows you to register your email address on a waitlist.
Joining the waitlist does not give you any rights to access a product, receive a service, or pay for anything. We will contact you when — and only if — we launch.

sections.heading: 2. Who you are contracting with
body: You are entering into these terms with Dmytro Fenko (publishing as Dmitry Fenko), a UK sole trader operating under the trading name SpeakMove. This is not a company. It is one person running a research and product-discovery project.

In accordance with the Companies Act 2006 and general UK transparency requirements: I am not incorporated as a limited company. There is no Companies House registration number. Address for service of documents: Weston-super-Mare, UK, BS23 1RH. Contact: hi@speakmove.app.

I intend to incorporate as a UK Limited Company before launching paid features. Until that happens, the individual named above is personally responsible for this service. If incorporation occurs, I will notify you by email at least 30 days in advance and you may object — including by requesting deletion of your data — before the transition takes effect.

sections.heading: 3. What you get
body: By using this website and joining the waitlist, you get:

items: Access to informational content about SpeakMove's planned features and pricing (this is not a binding offer).
A place on our email waitlist, meaning we will notify you when SpeakMove launches.
One email at launch. That is it — no ongoing marketing unless you separately opt in.

sections.heading: 4. What we do not promise
body: We do not guarantee:

items: That SpeakMove will launch, or when.
That you will receive access to any product.
That prices shown on the site will remain unchanged.
Any service level, uptime, or performance standard — the website is informational only.
That our Telegram bot will become functional — it currently only sends an automated pre-launch reply.

body: The pricing shown on this site is indicative and informational only. It does not constitute a binding commercial offer.

sections.heading: 5. Future terms for paid features (effective at launch)
body: This section sets out terms that will apply only when SpeakMove launches paid subscriptions and starts charging users. Until launch, no payments are processed and these terms are informational.

items: All prices displayed on the site are VAT-inclusive. The price you see is the price you pay; applicable VAT or sales tax is included.
Subscriptions can be cancelled at any time from within the bot. Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period and does not trigger any cancellation fee.

EU/UK consumers have a statutory 14-day cooling-off period after the initial subscription. A full refund will be issued upon request within this window, minus the monetary value of any cashback bonuses already redeemed.

Optional waiver of cooling-off (EU Consumer Rights Directive Article 16(m)): if the user expressly opts in at checkout to immediate commencement of digital services and expressly waives the right of withdrawal for digital content supplied with their prior express consent, the cooling-off right is forfeited upon the first successful charge. The user retains all other consumer rights, including the right to cancel future renewals at any time. Without this confirmation, payment cannot be completed via the waiver flow.
After the 14-day cooling-off, refunds for annual plans are issued pro-rata for full unused months only. Monthly subscriptions are non-refundable after the cooling-off window.
Cashback bonuses (SM) are a loyalty reward, not a financial instrument. They have no monetary value outside the platform and cannot be exchanged for fiat currency directly.
The maximum cashback that can accrue in any single calendar month is 30% of the user's monthly subscription price. Once reached, no further bonuses accrue until the next billing cycle.
Cashback bonuses expire 12 months from the date they are accrued. Expired bonuses cannot be redeemed.
Upon cancellation of a paid subscription, the user has 30 days to redeem any remaining bonuses. After this grace period, unredeemed bonuses are forfeited.
Minimum redemption thresholds: $5 for early access subscribers (first 200), $10 for standard subscribers.
Payment methods: bank card (via Stripe) or Telegram Stars. Stripe card payment is the primary checkout option.

body: We reserve the right to refuse cashback payouts where we have reasonable grounds to believe the user has engaged in fraud, multi-accounting, or abuse of the loyalty programme.

sections.heading: 6. Acceptable use
body: When using our website and waitlist, you must not:

items: Submit multiple email addresses to claim multiple waitlist places.
Use automated tools or bots to submit forms or scrape our site.
Attempt to gain unauthorised access to our systems.
Use our content or branding without permission.

body: We reserve the right to remove any waitlist entry we reasonably believe violates these rules.

sections.heading: 7. Intellectual property
body: All content on this website — including text, graphics, the SpeakMove name, logo, and brand — belongs to Dmytro Fenko, trading as SpeakMove, or is used with permission.
You may not copy, reproduce, or redistribute any of it without our written consent.

sections.heading: 8. Limitation of liability
body: This is a free, pre-launch informational service. To the fullest extent permitted by law:

items: We are not liable for any indirect, consequential, or special losses arising from your use of this website or waitlist.
Our total liability to you for any claim arising from this service is capped at £100.
Nothing in these terms limits liability for death or personal injury caused by negligence, or for fraud.

sections.heading: 9. Changes to these terms
body: We may update these terms at any time during the pre-launch phase. If we make material changes, we will notify waitlist members by email at least 14 days before they take effect.
If you do not agree with the new terms, you can remove yourself from the waitlist at any time by contacting hi@speakmove.app.

sections.heading: 10. Termination and unsubscribing
body: You can remove yourself from the waitlist at any time by clicking the unsubscribe link in any email we send, or by emailing hi@speakmove.app.
We can remove your waitlist entry at any time, for any reason, including if we decide not to launch or if we believe you have violated these terms. We will not charge you anything in either case.

sections.heading: 11. Future company migration
body: We intend to incorporate SpeakMove as a UK limited company before launching paid features. When this happens, the benefit and obligations of these terms will transfer to the successor entity.
We will give you at least 30 days' prior notice by email. You will have the right to opt out and have your data deleted before the transfer.

sections.heading: 12. Governing law and jurisdiction
body: These terms are governed by the laws of England and Wales. Any disputes will be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales.

If you are a consumer resident in an EU or EEA country, nothing in these terms affects your right to rely on mandatory consumer protection laws of your home country, or to bring proceedings in the courts of your home country, as provided by Article 6 of the Rome I Regulation (EU 593/2008) and equivalent rules.

sections.heading: 13. Contact
body: For any questions about these terms: hi@speakmove.app

meta.title: Cookie Policy
meta.intro: This page explains what cookies the SpeakMove website uses, why, and how you can control them.

sections.heading: 1. What are cookies?
body: Cookies are small text files that a website saves on your device when you visit. They help the site remember information between pages or visits — like whether you have already accepted this cookie notice.
Some cookies are set by us; others are set by third-party services we use (such as Google Analytics).

sections.heading: 2. Cookies we use
body: We use two categories of cookies:

items: Strictly necessary — these make the website work. We do not need your consent to set them.
Analytics — these help us understand how visitors use the site. We only set them after you accept analytics cookies.

rows: Stores your cookie consent choice so we do not ask again on every visit.
Maintains your session while you are on the site. Used for CSRF protection on form submissions.
Protects form submissions against cross-site request forgery attacks.
Identifies unique visitors to the site for Google Analytics 4.
Stores and counts page views and session data for Google Analytics 4.

sections.heading: 4. Third-party cookies
body: Google Analytics cookies (_ga, _ga_*) are set by Google LLC. Google processes analytics data in the US under the UK Extension to the EU–US Data Privacy Framework.
For more information on how Google uses data from sites that use its services, see: google.com/policies/privacy/partners

sections.heading: 5. How to control cookies
body: You have three ways to control cookies on this site:

items: Our cookie banner: when you first visit, you can accept all cookies, reject analytics cookies, or choose by category. Accepting and rejecting options are given equal prominence — there are no pre-ticked boxes.
Change your preference: you can update your choice at any time by clicking 'Cookie settings' in the footer.
Browser settings: most browsers let you block or delete cookies. Visit your browser's help pages for instructions. Note that blocking strictly necessary cookies may affect how the site works.

body: Withdrawing consent for analytics cookies stops new analytics data from being collected. It does not delete data already sent to Google Analytics before you withdrew.

sections.heading: 6. Changes to this policy
body: If we add new cookies or change how we use existing ones, we will update this page and, where required, ask for your consent again.

sections.heading: 7. Contact
body: Questions about our cookie use: hi@speakmove.app
